situation: Audit-2026-07-07-028 finding M-005-risk: validate_order (risk.rs:62) and revalidate_at_fill (risk.rs:178) both used `<=` against -daily_loss_limit, blocking buys when daily_pnl equals the threshold exactly. Industry convention for 'max loss = X' is allow up to X inclusive, block only strictly beyond. Existing test used -3500 vs -3000 limit so boundary was never exercised.
action: Changed both checks from `<=` to `<`. Added boundary test `daily_loss_limit_boundary_exact_threshold_passes` asserting pass at exact threshold (-3000) and fail one cent beyond (-3000.01). Single-file route-fix in risk.rs.
outcome_evidence: Commit 42f568d on auto/opt-risk-1783360526 pushed to origin. Diff: 2 operator changes (<= → <) + 22-line boundary test added. Branch URL: https://code.alibaba-inc.com/xtool/moni/codereview/new?from=master&to=auto/opt-risk-1783360526
next_suggestion: Next pending derived_fix: M-001-position (direction-shift, medium) — avg_cost includes buy-side fees causing misleading unrealized PnL. Touches position.rs public display semantics; opt-worktree judge_direction_kind will auto-open new worktree if position.rs is in public-interfaces.txt.

audit_findings.finding: Daily loss limit uses <= boundary — blocks buy at exact threshold, contradicting 'max loss' semantics
audit_findings.remediation: Change <= to < in both validate_order and revalidate_at_fill; add boundary test
